purpose: Rank repository evidence-closure and Platinum-gap workload. The score does not rank clinical efficacy, personal suitability, legal availability or product safety.
formula: Identity×4 + HumanEvidence×4 + Regulatory×3 + Safety×3 + SupplierEvidence×2 + AnalyticalFeasibility×2 + CommercialBoundary×2

T1.name: Primary-Source Closure Candidate
T1.rule: Score at least 70, canonical identity at least 4/5, and human evidence, regulatory closure and safety-source closure each at least 4/5.
T1.meaning: Best current candidates for full source extraction and Platinum-gap analysis. Not Platinum and not commercially released.

T2.name: Advanced Evidence Expansion
T2.rule: Score at least 60, canonical identity at least 3/5, human evidence at least 3/5 and regulatory closure at least 3/5.
T2.meaning: Substantial evidence exists, but source extraction, supplier verification or clinical/regulatory closure remains material.

T3.name: Substantial Closure Required
T3.rule: Score at least 45 or fails a T1/T2 maturity gate.
T3.meaning: Requires identity, supplier, safety, human-evidence or analytical work before Platinum-gap analysis is efficient.

T4.name: High Analytical or Evidence Burden
T4.meaning: Complex mixture, biologic, blend, weak evidence base or high verification burden.

Q.name: Identity / Composition Quarantine
Q.rule: Critical unresolved identity or composition control applies.
Q.meaning: No pharmacological, product-equivalence or commercial progression until the critical identity or composition defect is resolved.

mandatory_controls: No score establishes clinical efficacy or safety.
Critical identity/composition assets remain quarantined regardless of numeric score.
Human review is required before publication or commercial decisions.
